cap1.h2: Tell Gradvera the project type, and it proposes the line items similar projects usually need.
cap1.p: When you start a new estimate, Gradvera recognizes the project type and proposes the sections, materials, labor and subcontracted work typically required. The suggestions come from your firm's own past projects—not from a generic template.
cap1.step1d: See how similar estimates were organized.
cap1.step2d: Add what fits and build your estimate.
cap1.screen.structureSub: Define sections for an organised estimate. Reorder or rename later — nothing is locked.
cap1.screen.projSub: New estimate · Step 3 of 5 · Adding items
cap1.screen.banner: 20 items added with awaiting confirmation status
cap1.screen.bannerSub: They will sit at the bottom of the table until reviewed. Prices are suggested, not final.
cap1.screen.footPre: Suggested items will be added with
cap1.screen.footPost: status. Prices are suggested, not final.
cap2.h2: Make pricing decisions with greater certainty
cap2.p: Gradvera shows the projects, prices, and historical context behind every price recommendation. Estimators remain in control, and every decision they make sharpens Gradvera's next recommendation.
cap2.card1d: Confidence is calculated from the number and quality of matching projects within ±10%.
cap2.card2d: See how this price sits within the historical range for similar work in your company.
cap2.card3d: View the projects and data that contributed to this price.
cap2.card4d: Your decision is final. Every adjustment improves future suggestions.
cap2.point1d: Confidence is calculated from the number and quality of matching projects within ±10%.
cap2.point2d: See how this price sits within the historical range for similar work in your company.
cap2.point3d: View the projects and data that contributed to this price.
cap2.point4d: Your decision is final. Every adjustment improves future suggestions.
cap2.foot: Gradvera learns from your pricing decisions and gets smarter over time.
